name: skill-curation
description: Review the skill-curate health report and classify each flagged skill as keep, promote, merge, deprecate, or retire. Review-only; skill changes route through the stage machine.
rubric: Every skill flagged by harness:skill:curate (orphan/stale/metadata-invalid/deprecated-but-referenced/high-drift) has an explicit decision (keep/promote/merge/deprecate/retire).
Each decision entry in .github/harness/memory/curation/ cites the scanner finding(s) that prompted it.
Decisions that change a skill (merge/retire/rewrite/promote) name target files and a follow-up task title with explicit Understand -> Architect routing before code changes.
No skill is edited, merged, or deleted inside this loop.
Multi-root drift decisions distinguish intentional divergence from real drift (only slugs present in 2+ roots can drift).
fixPrompt: Run `npm run harness:skill:curate -- --json`. For each flagged skill, create or update an entry in .github/harness/memory/curation/ with a decision (keep/promote/merge/deprecate/retire), rationale, and a citation of the scanner finding. If the decision changes a skill, define a follow-up task title, target files/skills, and explicit Understand -> Architect routing. Do not edit or delete skills in this loop.
guardrails: Do not edit, merge, rewrite, or delete skills directly in this loop; triage and route only.
Do not auto-edit metadata status; propose and apply it as a normal change through the stage machine.
Do not treat intentional multi-root divergence as drift; only slugs present in 2+ roots can drift.
Do not leave a scanner-flagged skill without a decision after loop completion.
onExhausted: Stop. Report skills that remain undecided, why they are blocked, and what evidence is needed for a final decision.
